stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 33% and 48%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $130K in good weather patterns, $35K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $137K in good weather patterns, $32K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $273K in good weather patterns, $65K in bad weather patterns. corn: $210K in good weather patterns, $113K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.41: best option is corn with EV=$152.8K. The optimal choice switches: corn at P(good)=0.33, soybeans at P(good)=0.48. EV range of best option: [145.0, 164.8].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: corn at P(good)=0.33, soybeans at P(good)=0.48.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
